@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
 {"id":"bd-1a2","title":"EPIC: Provider Writers (Export to Native Format)","description":"Implement writers that convert from canonical IR into each provider's native session format.

This is the novel part of casr: CASS reads sessions, but casr must write provider-plausible sessions that the target tool can resume.

Target outputs:
- Claude Code: JSONL at `~/.claude/projects/<hash>/<session-id>.jsonl`
- Codex: JSONL at `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-N.jsonl`
- Gemini CLI: JSON at `~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/session-<id>.json`

Critical architecture constraint:
- Reuse CASS reader knowledge by vendoring/adapting relevant logic into casr.
- Do NOT introduce a runtime dependency on CASS.

Writer quality bar:
1. Output files must be accepted by provider resume flows in real smoke tests.
2. Preserve maximal fidelity from canonical IR, including provider-specific extras when possible.
3. Use atomic write semantics and deterministic serialization choices where practical.
4. Include explicit rollback behavior on verification failure (no partial broken sessions left behind).
5. Emit rich trace diagnostics explaining field mapping decisions and generated IDs/paths.

## Success Criteria

- Each core provider writer passes:
  - same-provider read-back verification,
  - golden-output conformance suite,
  - (opt-in) real-provider acceptance smoke harness.
- Writer output is deterministic where expected (excluding generated ids/timestamps with documented normalization rules).
- `--force` overwrite is always recoverable via backups and is well logged.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:02.674259111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:00:00.512491489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.697242211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:00:29.457808322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1a2.1","title":"Implement Claude Code session writer","description":"Implement Provider::write_session() for Claude Code in src/providers/claude_code.rs.

Objective:
Produce Claude-native session artifacts that are resume-acceptable and structurally close to real Claude Code outputs, using findings from bd-1a2.4.

Output target:
~/.claude/projects/<project-dir-key>/<new-session-id>.jsonl

Writer requirements:
1. Compute project directory key from workspace path using the verified algorithm from bd-1a2.4.
2. Generate a new session ID and ensure filename/session fields are consistent with Claude resume behavior.
3. Emit user/assistant events in Claude-compatible JSONL structure:
   - first user entry carries cwd/sessionId/gitBranch when required,
   - subsequent entries use minimal native shape,
   - assistant entries include model metadata when available.
4. Serialize timestamps to provider-acceptable ISO-8601.
5. Preserve high-value provider extras when safe (without violating native format expectations).
6. Use atomic write semantics (bd-ikb.3) including conflict + backup behavior.
7. Ensure output path creation is robust when directories are missing.

Validation requirements:
- Must pass same-provider read-back verification (casr Claude reader parses written output).
- Must pass golden-output conformance tests against redacted-real Claude fixture expectations (structure + required fields).
- Must pass opt-in real-provider acceptance smoke when Claude CLI is installed (bd-24z.13).

Acceptance criteria:
- Written Claude session can be resumed using the documented Claude resume command with generated ID.
- Project directory key and session ID behavior match documented research findings.
- Reader round-trip and golden-output conformance tests pass.
- Conflict/force/backup behavior is covered by tests and logged clearly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:17.382537275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:07:30.363345777Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.027967289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.696760305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.547792525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.622855484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:52.094561565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

   - first user entry carries cwd/sessionId/gitBranch when required,

4. Serialize timestamps to provider-acceptable ISO-8601.

6. Use atomic write semantics (bd-ikb.3) including conflict + backup behavior.
7. Ensure output path creation is robust when directories are missing.

Validation requirements:
- Must pass same-provider read-back verification (casr Claude reader parses written output).
- Must pass golden-output conformance tests against redacted-real Claude fixture expectations (structure + required fields).
- Must pass opt-in real-provider acceptance smoke when Claude CLI is installed (bd-24z.13).

Acceptance criteria:
- Written Claude session can be resumed using the documented Claude resume command with generated ID.
- Project directory key and session ID behavior match documented research findings.
- Reader round-trip and golden-output conformance tests pass.
- Conflict/force/backup behavior is covered by tests and logged clearly.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:17.382537275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:31.833561293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.027967289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.696760305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.547792525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.622855484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:52.094561565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.2","title":"Implement Codex session writer","description":"Implement Provider::write_session() for Codex in src/providers/codex.rs.

Objective:
Generate Codex-compatible rollout artifacts that are accepted by Codex resume flows, based on bd-1a2.5 research outcomes.

Output target:
~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-N.jsonl (or documented equivalent if research proves a stricter requirement).

Writer requirements:
1. Determine session file placement and external session identifier rules using bd-1a2.5 findings (do not assume path-only or UUID-only without evidence).
2. Emit Codex-native event envelopes in correct sequence:
   - session_meta (workspace/cwd context)
   - response_item events for conversation turns
   - event_msg token_count entries when token usage is available
3. Preserve role/content fidelity and map canonical assistant/user roles correctly.
4. Preserve key provider extras when compatible with Codex schema.
5. Serialize timestamps to provider-acceptable format.
6. Use atomic write semantics (bd-ikb.3), including conflict detection and backup handling.

Validation requirements:
- Must pass same-provider read-back verification with casr Codex reader.
- Must pass golden-output conformance tests against redacted-real Codex fixture expectations.
- Must pass opt-in real-provider acceptance smoke when Codex CLI is installed (bd-24z.13).

Acceptance criteria:
- Output file/event structure matches documented Codex format expectations.
- Resume identifier behavior is implemented per verified research (and documented).
- Reader round-trip + golden-output conformance tests pass.
- Conflict/force/backup behavior is validated in tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:27.646685290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:07:39.030118544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.826700675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.918521376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.770791052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.843932795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:52.172838396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.3","title":"Implement Gemini CLI session writer","description":"Implement Provider::write_session() for Gemini CLI in src/providers/gemini.rs.

Objective:
Generate Gemini session JSON artifacts that are accepted by Gemini session discovery/resume flows, based on bd-1a2.6 findings.

Output target:
~/.gemini/tmp/<project-hash-or-equivalent>/chats/session-<id>.json

Writer requirements:
1. Determine project hash / directory derivation and session ID conventions from verified research.
2. Emit Gemini-native top-level structure (sessionId, projectHash-equivalent, start/lastUpdated, messages array).
3. Map assistant turns to Gemini message type "model" (not "assistant") and preserve user/model ordering.
4. Preserve grounding/citation metadata and multipart content blocks when present.
5. Serialize timestamps in accepted format and maintain chronological coherence.
6. Create required directory hierarchy when absent.
7. Use atomic write semantics (bd-ikb.3), including conflict and backup behavior.

Validation requirements:
- Must pass same-provider read-back verification with casr Gemini reader.
- Must pass golden-output conformance tests against redacted-real Gemini fixture expectations.
- Must pass opt-in real-provider acceptance smoke when Gemini CLI is installed (bd-24z.13).

Acceptance criteria:
- Written session is discoverable/resumable by Gemini according to documented research.
- Hash/directory/session-ID behavior follows verified rules.
- Reader round-trip + golden-output conformance tests pass.
- Conflict/force/backup behavior is covered by tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:37.524099794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:07:50.126050613Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gemini","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.625121927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:40.159898412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:39.993120897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:40.080470904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:52.250813912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.4","title":"Reverse-engineer Claude Code session directory naming","description":"Investigate and document how Claude Code derives the project directory key under ~/.claude/projects/ and how resume identity maps to files.

Research method:
1. Correlate existing ~/.claude/projects/* directories with known workspace paths.
2. Inspect Claude CLI/package behavior and source (if available).
3. Cross-check with CASS Claude connector assumptions and explicitly note matches/differences.
4. Validate with synthetic write + real Claude resume attempt.

Required outputs:
- Verified algorithm: workspace path -> project directory key.
- Verified session filename/ID behavior used by resume.
- Determination of whether additional manifests/index files are required for resume discovery.
- Rust helper implementation in casr with tests against multiple real samples.
- Documentation in src/providers/claude_code.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Algorithm validated against at least 3 real workspace/project-dir pairs.
- Helper function reproduces all sampled keys deterministically.
- Resume ID/file mapping is documented with concrete examples.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:43:50.559354910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.862303901Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.862285587Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered and implemented project_dir_key(): all non-alphanumeric chars → hyphens. Validated against real ~/.claude/projects/ directories. Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.023460308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.104491128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.5","title":"Reverse-engineer Codex session resume mechanism","description":"Investigate and document Codex resume resolution semantics: how `codex resume <id>` maps IDs to session files and which file fields are mandatory.

Research method:
1. Inspect Codex CLI help/behavior and source where available.
2. Probe ~/.codex/sessions structure across multiple real sessions.
3. Create controlled synthetic session files and test acceptance/rejection.
4. Compare findings against CASS Codex connector assumptions and document deltas.

Required outputs:
- Verified lookup algorithm (path-based, embedded ID-based, index-based, or hybrid).
- Minimum required JSONL/JSON fields for resume acceptance.
- Confirmation of any checksum/signature/index requirements.
- Concrete mapping examples from observed session IDs to file paths.
- Documentation in src/providers/codex.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Resume lookup behavior is validated empirically with real/synthetic cases.
- Mandatory vs optional fields are clearly listed.
- Research findings directly inform writer implementation rules in bd-1a2.2.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:03.752649488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.959813778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.959787269Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered Codex session resume: ULID session IDs, rollout-TIMESTAMP-ID.jsonl naming, session_meta payload.id, date-hierarchy directories. rollout_path() helper + owns_session with 3-tier lookup (path, filename suffix, body). Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:07.824387932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.181214064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.6","title":"Reverse-engineer Gemini CLI session resume mechanism","description":"Investigate and document Gemini session discovery/resume semantics, including session ID mapping, hash directory derivation, and minimum acceptable file shape.

Research method:
1. Inspect Gemini CLI commands/help and source where available.
2. Examine ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats layout and any related state/index files.
3. Create synthetic sessions and validate acceptance/rejection paths.
4. Compare findings with CASS Gemini connector assumptions and document deltas.

Required outputs:
- Verified resume/discovery mechanism (explicit resume command vs implicit discovery behavior).
- Verified relationship between sessionId, filename, and hash-directory placement.
- Minimum required JSON fields and any required sibling artifacts.
- Documentation in src/providers/gemini.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Gemini acceptance behavior is validated with reproducible experiments.
- Hash/ID/path rules are documented with concrete examples.
- Research findings directly inform writer implementation rules in bd-1a2.3.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:14.313844502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:56.055019514Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:56.054999035Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered Gemini session resume: SHA256(workspace) project hash, session-TIMESTAMP-UUIDprefix.json naming, sessionId in body. project_hash() and session_filename() helpers. Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gemini","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:07.625103399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.258155228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg","title":"EPIC: CLI Interface & UX","description":"Implement the casr CLI using clap.

Primary usage pattern: `casr <target-agent> resume <session-id>`.

Target agents:
- `cc` (Claude Code)
- `cod` (Codex)
- `gmi` (Gemini CLI)

Additional subcommands:
- `casr list` (list sessions across providers)
- `casr info <session-id>` (inspect a session without converting)
- `casr providers` (detection status + evidence)
- `casr completions <shell>`

The CLI must:
- auto-detect the source provider from the session ID (with explicit ambiguity errors)
- show clear progress + actionable failures
- report success with the exact resume command for the target provider
- support `--json` output for automation

Uses color with TTY detection; colors must be disabled automatically for `--json` and non-TTY output.

## Success Criteria

- Primary `resume` flow is fast, safe (atomic writes + backup), and self-verifying.
- `list/info/providers` are useful for discovery and debugging without conversion.
- All commands support `--json` with stable schemas and contract tests.
- Error messages include remediation hints (install, `--source`, `--force`, etc.).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:09.242303221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:52:21.915103800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.135990126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.056337066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1kg.1","title":"Implement CLI argument parsing with clap","description":"Implement the clap CLI in `src/main.rs`. Use clap derive macros.

## Commands

Usage patterns:

- `casr <target> resume <session-id>` (primary)
- `casr list [--provider <slug>] [--workspace <path>] [--limit <n>] [--sort <field>]`
- `casr info <session-id>`
- `casr providers`
- `casr completions <bash|zsh|fish>`
- `casr --version`
- `casr --help`

Where `<target>` is one of: `cc` (Claude Code), `cod` (Codex), `gmi` (Gemini CLI).

## Global Flags

- `--dry-run` : show what would happen without writing.
- `--force` : overwrite existing target session if it exists (creates backups).
- `--json` : machine-readable JSON output (no ANSI color, stable field names).
- `--verbose` : enable debug-level logs.
- `--trace` : enable trace-level logs.
- `--source <alias_or_path>` : pin source provider alias (`cc|cod|gmi`) OR a direct native session file path.
- `--enrich` : opt-in enrichment messages (bd-ikb.4).

Logging note:
- `--verbose`/`--trace` must configure the tracing subscriber filter directly (do not claim to "set RUST_LOG" at runtime).
- If `RUST_LOG` is already set, `--verbose`/`--trace` should override only for the `casr` target (documented precedence).

## Acceptance Criteria

- clap surface matches the README command reference.
- `--source` accepts either a known alias or a path; invalid values produce actionable usage errors.
- `--json` mode is supported across commands and disables color automatically.
- `--verbose` and `--trace` are wired to tracing subscriber filtering (bd-2zw.6).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:48.436160082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:09.536261776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.421300610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.554982969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1kg.1","title":"Implement CLI argument parsing with clap","description":"Implement the clap CLI in `src/main.rs`. Use clap derive macros.

## Commands

Usage patterns:

- `casr <target> resume <session-id>` (primary)
- `casr list [--provider <slug>] [--workspace <path>] [--limit <n>] [--sort <field>]`
- `casr info <session-id>`
- `casr providers`
- `casr completions <bash|zsh|fish>`
- `casr --version`
- `casr --help`

Where `<target>` is one of: `cc` (Claude Code), `cod` (Codex), `gmi` (Gemini CLI).

## Global Flags

- `--dry-run` : show what would happen without writing.
- `--force` : overwrite existing target session if it exists (creates backups).
- `--json` : machine-readable JSON output (no ANSI color, stable field names).
- `--verbose` : enable debug-level logs.
- `--trace` : enable trace-level logs.
- `--source <alias_or_path>` : pin source provider alias (`cc|cod|gmi`) OR a direct native session file path.
- `--enrich` : opt-in enrichment messages (bd-ikb.4).

Logging note:
- `--verbose`/`--trace` must configure the tracing subscriber filter directly (do not claim to "set RUST_LOG" at runtime).
- If `RUST_LOG` is already set, `--verbose`/`--trace` should override only for the `casr` target (documented precedence).

## Acceptance Criteria

- clap surface matches the README command reference.
- `--source` accepts either a known alias or a path; invalid values produce actionable usage errors.
- `--json` mode is supported across commands and disables color automatically.
- `--verbose` and `--trace` are wired to tracing subscriber filtering (bd-2zw.6).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:48.436160082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619805733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619702089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.421300610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.554982969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.2","title":"Implement resume command output and UX","description":"Implement the user-facing output for `casr <target> resume <session-id>`. This is what the user sees when they run the tool.

Success output example:

  ✓ Found session 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
    Source: Codex (codex)
    Workspace: /data/projects/myapp
    Messages: 47 (23 user, 24 assistant)
    Duration: 2h 15m

  ✓ Converted to Claude Code format
    Written to: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/new-session-id.jsonl
    Verified: read-back check passed ✓

  Resume with:
    claude --resume new-session-id

Backup notification (when `--force` was used and an existing session was backed up):

  ⚠ Existing session backed up to ~/.claude/projects/abc123/old-session-id.jsonl.bak

Error output examples:

  ✗ Session ID 'bad-id' not found
    Searched: Claude Code (roots: 2, files: 23), Codex (roots: 1, files: 156), Gemini (roots: 1, files: 12)
    Tip: Run `casr list` to see available sessions
    Tip: Use `--source <provider>` if you know the origin

  ✗ Target provider not writable
    Provider: Claude Code
    Evidence: ~/.claude/projects is read-only
    Tip: Fix permissions or set `CLAUDE_HOME` to a writable location

  ✗ Session already exists in target
    Existing: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/existing-session-id.jsonl (47 messages, 2h ago)
    Use `--force` to overwrite (creates .bak), or resume existing:
      claude --resume existing-session-id

  ✗ Write verification failed (this is a bug in casr)
    The written file could not be read back. Please report this with `--trace --json` output.

UX requirements:
- Use `colored` crate for colors (TTY-aware).
- Show progress for large sessions.
- In `--json` mode, output structured JSON instead of human text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Success output includes: source provider, workspace, message counts, written paths, and resume command.
- Warnings (validation warnings, binary-missing warnings) are visible in human output and present in `--json` result.
- Errors are actionable and include remediation hints.
- Output formatting is deterministic enough for e2e tests (bd-24z.5/bd-24z.6).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:00.316169403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:53:19.564726546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.674318857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.623081923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:16.265126132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.3","title":"Implement list and info subcommands","description":"Implement the `casr list` and `casr info <session-id>` subcommands.

## `casr list`

Scan all readable providers and display a table of sessions:

  Provider     Session ID                              Workspace                    Date          Messages
  claude-code  40f2cb68-fed7-4cee-83de-2b63ba9b7813   /data/projects/myapp         2026-02-07    47
  codex        019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b   /data/projects/other         2026-02-06    156
  gemini       3bdd5aeb-a260-4e20-94a3-3fa5a0b8f7b8   /data/projects/webapp        2026-02-05    247

Optional filters:
- `--provider <slug>` (only one provider)
- `--workspace <path>` (filter by workspace)
- `--limit <n>`
- `--sort <field>` (date/messages/provider)

## `casr info <session-id>`

Display details without converting:

  Session: 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
  Provider: Codex
  Workspace: /data/projects/other
  Started: 2026-02-06 14:30:00 UTC
  Ended: 2026-02-06 16:45:00 UTC
  Duration: 2h 15m
  Messages: 156 (78 user, 78 assistant)
  Model: o3-pro
  Source: ~/.codex/sessions/2026/02/06/rollout-1.jsonl

  First message: "Help me implement the auth module..."
  Last message: "All tests passing. Ready to deploy."

In `--json` mode, output structured JSON. These commands read from disk without converting.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `list` and `info` operate on providers with readable roots (binary not required).
- `list` supports filtering/sorting/limit as documented.
- `info` uses bd-3my.2 to resolve session ids and surfaces `AmbiguousSessionId` when applicable.
- `--json` output is schema-tested (bd-24z.11).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:12.866806438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:53:34.978169849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:15.105850276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.768209616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.838055419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.4","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"Use `clap_complete` to generate shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish. Add a `casr completions <shell>` subcommand that writes completion scripts to stdout.

Usage example:

```bash
eval "$(casr completions bash)"
```

Implementation notes:
- `clap_complete` integrates with clap derive: add dependency and generator wiring.
- Completions must include all subcommands, flags, and arguments.
- Provider aliases (`cc`, `cod`, `gmi`) must be offered as completions for `<target>`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `casr completions bash|zsh|fish` prints valid completion scripts.
- Completion scripts include all global flags and subcommands.
- Provider alias completions are correct and tested in a CLI integration test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:21.522469158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:53:41.397131084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:14.498347652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:25.201245070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ikb","title":"EPIC: Conversion Pipeline & Orchestration","description":"Wire up the complete conversion pipeline:

`detect/resolve source` → `read native` → `canonical IR` → `validate` → `write target native` → `verify read-back` → `print resume command`.

This is the orchestration layer that ties readers, writers, and discovery together. Must handle:
- validation (is the source session complete enough to convert?)
- conflict detection + `--force` backup semantics
- atomic writes (temp-then-rename)
- rollback on failure (including verify failures)
- progress reporting and rich logs
- dry-run mode (no filesystem writes)

## Success Criteria

- One command converts and prints the exact target resume command.
- No conversion leaves partial artifacts behind; `--force` is always recoverable via backups.
- Writers are verified via read-back before reporting success.
- Errors are actionable and debuggable from logs/artifacts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:16.762956837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:52:13.683179027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.900040558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.800373647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.978876910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.1","title":"Implement conversion pipeline orchestrator","description":"Implement the core conversion pipeline in `src/pipeline.rs`. This is the GENERIC orchestrator that works through the `Provider` trait; it must not depend on concrete provider implementations. Concrete providers are wired in by the integration task (bd-ikb.5).

## Core API

```rust
pub struct ConversionPipeline {
    registry: ProviderRegistry,
}

pub struct ConvertOptions {
    pub dry_run: bool,
    pub force: bool,
    pub source_hint: Option<String>, // alias or path, see bd-3my.2
}

pub struct ConversionResult {
    pub source_provider: String,
    pub target_provider: String,
    pub canonical_session: CanonicalSession,
    pub written: Option<WrittenSession>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}

impl ConversionPipeline {
    pub fn convert(
        &self,
        target_alias: &str,
        session_id: &str,
        opts: ConvertOptions,
    ) -> anyhow::Result<ConversionResult>;
}
```

## Pipeline Steps

1. Resolve the target provider from CLI alias (`cc`/`cod`/`gmi`) via the registry.
2. Validate target provider is writable/creatable (`DetectionResult.can_write()`).
   - If binary is missing but roots are writable, proceed and add a WARNING (user can install later).
3. Resolve the source provider + native path via bd-3my.2 (supports `--source <alias>` and `--source <path>`).
   - Must surface `AmbiguousSessionId` deterministically when multiple providers match.
4. Read source session into canonical IR via `source_provider.read_session(path)`.
5. Validate canonical session (bd-ikb.2). Stop only on validation errors; return warnings/info.
6. If `dry_run`, stop here (no writes) and return `written=None`.
7. Write via `target_provider.write_session(&canonical, WriteOptions { force })`.
   - Writers MUST use `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3) for conflict/backup semantics.
   - The pipeline must not pre-check conflicts (avoid TOCTOU); it should map `SessionConflict` errors to UX/JSON.
8. VERIFY: Read back the written artifact(s) via `target_provider.read_session(written_path)` and ensure parse + core invariants hold.
   - Emit trace logs comparing message count/order/roles and timestamp deltas.
9. On verification failure:
   - restore any backups created during the write (never leave user worse off),
   - remove newly written broken artifacts,
   - return `VerifyFailed`.

## Observability Requirements

- Wrap major stages in tracing spans: detect/resolve, read, validate, write, verify.
- Emit timings per stage.
- Include source/target slugs, source session id, and all written paths in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pipeline is provider-generic and fully unit-testable with mock providers (bd-24z.9).
- Pipeline uses bd-3my.2 for source resolution and propagates `AmbiguousSessionId`.
- Target writability is enforced; missing binary is a WARNING when write is possible (not a hard error).
- Conflict handling is centralized in `atomic_write`/writers; pipeline does not duplicate pre-checks.
- Verification failures perform rollback/restore of backups and return a distinct `VerifyFailed` error.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:26.461009045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:49:05.192018833Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:46.088665379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:55.934912155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.220650550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:46.000179002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.2","title":"Implement session validation and completeness checks","description":"Implement validation logic in the conversion pipeline that checks whether a canonical session is complete enough to be meaningfully converted. Runs between the read and write steps.

Validation checks (in order):

ERRORS (pipeline stops):
1. Session must have at least 1 message (empty sessions are useless).
2. Session must have at least 1 user message AND 1 assistant message.

WARNINGS (pipeline continues; surfaced in UX and `--json`):
3. Session has no workspace (target agent may not know what project to work in).
4. Session has no timestamps (ordering may be unreliable).
5. Messages have unusual role ordering (e.g., two user messages in a row) — valid but may confuse the target.
6. Very short session (<3 messages) may not provide enough context.
7. High malformed/skipped rate in source parse (indicates possible corruption).

INFO (shown only in verbose/trace and stored in `ConversionResult.warnings/info`):
8. Session has tool calls/results (may not translate perfectly between providers).
9. Provider-specific metadata likely to be lost in conversion.
10. Tool call/result pairing issues (unmatched call ids).

Return `ValidationResult { errors, warnings, info }`.

Design principle: permissive rather than strict. A session that's good enough should convert, even if some metadata is lost.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Validation checks are fully unit-tested (bd-24z.9) and warnings/info are surfaced deterministically.
- Validation never blocks conversion for non-critical issues; it warns instead.
- Validation output is included in `--json` conversion results.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:38.039191491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:53:51.467169603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:00.938320509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.470558755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ikb.3","title":"Implement atomic file writing with temp-then-rename","description":"Implement safe file writing for the conversion pipeline. When writing a converted session to the target provider's directory, casr must never leave partial/corrupt files or silently destroy an existing session.

## Atomic Write Strategy

Implement a utility used by all writers:

```rust
pub struct AtomicWriteOutcome {
    pub target_path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub temp_path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub backup_path: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub fn atomic_write(
    target_path: &std::path::Path,
    bytes: &[u8],
    force: bool,
) -> Result<AtomicWriteOutcome, CasrError>;

pub fn restore_backup(
    outcome: &AtomicWriteOutcome,
) -> Result<(), CasrError>; // best-effort restore when verify fails
```

Steps:
1. Create parent directories (`create_dir_all`).
2. Create temp file in same directory (e.g., `.casr-tmp-<random>`).
3. Write bytes, flush, and `sync_all()`.
4. Conflict handling:
   - If target exists and `!force`: return `SessionConflict` (include existing path).
   - If target exists and `force`: create a backup first (append `.bak`, with deterministic de-dupe like `.bak.1`, `.bak.2` if needed).
     - Prefer atomic rename for backup when possible.
5. Atomically rename temp -> target (`std::fs::rename` on same filesystem).

Cleanup/rollback rules:
- If any step fails before temp->target rename: delete temp file.
- If backup was created but the final rename fails: restore the backup into place.
- On post-write verification failure (bd-ikb.1): restore backup (if any) and remove the newly written broken target.

Edge cases to handle and test:
- read-only directory, permission denied, disk full, path too long.
- symlink targets (do not accidentally overwrite through unexpected symlink chains).
- Windows rename semantics (best-effort; if atomic rename isn't available, still guarantee "no partial writes").

## Acceptance Criteria

- `atomic_write` guarantees "either old target remains OR new target is fully written".
- `--force` always produces a recoverable backup path (reported to UX via `WrittenSession.backup_paths`).
- Failure injection suite (bd-24z.12) can deterministically simulate failure at each stage and assert cleanup.
- Verification failure path restores backups and removes broken outputs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:47.332924577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:49:24.076384188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:01.014390863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.267306103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ikb.3","title":"Implement atomic file writing with temp-then-rename","description":"Implement safe file writing for the conversion pipeline. When writing a converted session to the target provider's directory, casr must never leave partial/corrupt files or silently destroy an existing session.

## Atomic Write Strategy

Implement a utility used by all writers:

```rust
pub struct AtomicWriteOutcome {
    pub target_path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub temp_path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub backup_path: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub fn atomic_write(
    target_path: &std::path::Path,
    bytes: &[u8],
    force: bool,
) -> Result<AtomicWriteOutcome, CasrError>;

pub fn restore_backup(
    outcome: &AtomicWriteOutcome,
) -> Result<(), CasrError>; // best-effort restore when verify fails
```

Steps:
1. Create parent directories (`create_dir_all`).
2. Create temp file in same directory (e.g., `.casr-tmp-<random>`).
3. Write bytes, flush, and `sync_all()`.
4. Conflict handling:
   - If target exists and `!force`: return `SessionConflict` (include existing path).
   - If target exists and `force`: create a backup first (append `.bak`, with deterministic de-dupe like `.bak.1`, `.bak.2` if needed).
     - Prefer atomic rename for backup when possible.
5. Atomically rename temp -> target (`std::fs::rename` on same filesystem).

Cleanup/rollback rules:
- If any step fails before temp->target rename: delete temp file.
- If backup was created but the final rename fails: restore the backup into place.
- On post-write verification failure (bd-ikb.1): restore backup (if any) and remove the newly written broken target.

Edge cases to handle and test:
- read-only directory, permission denied, disk full, path too long.
- symlink targets (do not accidentally overwrite through unexpected symlink chains).
- Windows rename semantics (best-effort; if atomic rename isn't available, still guarantee "no partial writes").

## Acceptance Criteria

- `atomic_write` guarantees "either old target remains OR new target is fully written".
- `--force` always produces a recoverable backup path (reported to UX via `WrittenSession.backup_paths`).
- Failure injection suite (bd-24z.12) can deterministically simulate failure at each stage and assert cleanup.
- Verification failure path restores backups and removes broken outputs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:47.332924577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:16.790463676Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:16.790385850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:01.014390863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.267306103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.4","title":"Implement session context enrichment for better resumption","description":"When converting a session from provider A to provider B, the target agent may benefit from additional context explaining that a conversion happened. This feature adds optional enrichment to the conversion pipeline.

Enrichment options (opt-in via `--enrich`):
1. Prepend a system message: `This session was originally created in [Provider A] and converted to [Provider B] format by casr. The original session ID was [id]. Some provider-specific context may have been lost in conversion.`
2. Preserve workspace context: if the source session referenced specific files, include a short orientation blurb.
3. Add a synthetic summary message of where the original session left off (useful if it ended mid-task).
4. Include a short conversation summary of the last N messages.

Requirements:
- Enrichment must be clearly distinguishable from real conversation (metadata flag + obvious prefix) so it does not confuse the target agent.
- Enrichment must never run by default.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `--enrich` produces deterministic, clearly marked synthetic messages.
- Enrichment does not break provider-native writer acceptance (covered by round-trip + conformance tests).
- Enrichment behavior is unit-tested (pipeline tests) and covered in e2e script.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:52.620517770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:54:13.302850956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enrichment","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:14.701343864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:57.843666923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.5","title":"Integration: Wire all providers into registry and run smoke test","description":"This is the full integration convergence task after core readers/writers/pipeline are implemented.

Scope:
1. Build default provider registry wiring for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini.
2. Connect registry + conversion pipeline into CLI dispatch paths.
3. Run full cross-provider integration matrix (all 6 conversion directions):
   - CC->Codex, CC->Gemini, Codex->CC, Codex->Gemini, Gemini->CC, Gemini->Codex
4. For each matrix case, assert:
   - conversion succeeds,
   - output artifact exists and parses,
   - resume command is generated correctly,
   - read-back verification succeeds.
5. Ensure behavior is graceful when a provider is not installed (registry remains constructable and diagnostics are actionable).

Validation requirements:
- Emit structured integration logs per path (source, target, session id, output path, timings).
- Produce artifact bundle on failure (source fixture, generated output, diff/diagnostic summary).
- Ensure this task unblocks e2e smoke and UX output tasks with confidence.

Acceptance criteria:
- All six conversion paths pass integration tests in isolated temp homes.
- Missing-provider scenarios are tested and produce actionable errors.
- Integration logs and artifacts are sufficiently detailed to debug failures without rerunning interactively.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:05.421856359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:20.730975497Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","integration","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:10.957400557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:11.033879732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:11.109976821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:10.709016025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:10.789237658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:10.877036388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:07.420902722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:10.633170758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.6","title":"Handle same-provider conversion gracefully","description":"Handle the edge case where the user runs `casr cc resume <claude-session-id>`: converting a session back to the same provider. This is not an error, but it is a special case.

Smart default behavior:
1. If source and target are the same provider and the session already exists at the expected native path, treat it as a no-op lookup: print resume command and exit.
2. If source and target are the same provider but the session is in a different location/shape (e.g., non-standard path via `--source`), treat it as a copy operation (read -> canonical -> write) under a new id.
3. Always print a notice: `Note: source and target are both <Provider>.`

Rationale:
- user doesn't remember which provider a session came from
- user wants the resume command without conversion
- user wants to duplicate/move a session inside a provider

Implementation sketch:
- In `ConversionPipeline::convert()`, detect `source.slug == target.slug` and choose between no-op vs copy depending on whether write is necessary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Same-provider no-op path produces correct resume command without writes.
- Same-provider copy path produces a new session artifact under the provider's native store.
- Behavior is covered by pipeline unit tests (bd-24z.9) and e2e script (bd-24z.6).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:10.140744557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:54:01.940201199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.646929414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:14.399844997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
